brief_justification: Paper A studies a singularly-perturbed second-order Modica-Mortola functional and proves Gamma-convergence to an interfacial energy for laminate configurations (calculus-of-variations / phase-transition asymptotics with rank-one connections), whereas paper B builds a depth-separable neural network for mixed-frequency forecasting and derives a non-asymptotic prediction error bound (statistical learning / function-approximation theory). The governing machinery differs entirely — energy minimization and Gamma-limits versus neural approximation and generalization bounds — with no coherent role-for-role correspondence, only the generic overlap of being mathematical analysis.
